events.change_summary: Intent check: Enable brew install via release CI and Homebrew tap

events.summary: Intent check pushed — two-part Homebrew pipeline: tag-triggered CI publishes macOS arm64/x86_64 binaries to GitHub Releases, then a public tap formula consumes those artifacts so `brew install` works end-to-end.

events.change_summary: Full scope: release CI pipeline with automated formula checksum update, 1 phase, bug-fix workflow

events.rationale: Fixes broken brew install behavior caused by missing CI and placeholder SHA256s — no new user-visible features (skip design), well-contained to 2 files in a single CI subsystem (skip architecture)

events.decision: Use native macOS runners per target architecture rather than cross-compilation
events.rationale: macos-14 (ARM) builds aarch64-apple-darwin natively; macos-13 (Intel) builds x86_64-apple-darwin natively. Avoids linker configuration, the `cross` crate, and SDK path complexity that frequently breaks in CI. Trade-off: two runner-minutes of wall time per release instead of one.

events.decision: Automate formula SHA256 update inside the release workflow rather than leaving it as a manual post-release step
events.rationale: The placeholder checksums are the root cause of the install failure. A manual update step would require the developer to remember to run it after every release and would leave the formula broken in the window between the release and the update. Automating via `sed` in the publish job eliminates that gap. Requires `contents: write` workflow permission and a direct commit to master.

events.decision: Keep Formula/setups.rb in the main setups repo rather than creating a separate homebrew-setups tap repo
events.rationale: The formula file already exists in this repo. Creating a separate homebrew-setups repo is additive infrastructure work outside the scope of fixing the broken install. The trade-off is that the short-form `brew tap holynakamoto/setups` command (which maps to homebrew-setups by convention) won't work; users need the full-URL form. Flagged in Out of Scope and Technical Notes as a natural follow-up.

events.summary: Full scope written (revision 2). Root cause: Formula/setups.rb has placeholder SHA256s and no .github/ directory exists, so no binaries are ever published. Fix: one phase — create .github/workflows/release.yml (parallel native macOS builds for arm64 and x86_64, automated formula checksum update via sed, GitHub Release creation) and remove placeholder comments from the formula. Workflow classified as bug-fix/standard with no architecture or design stages. Three decisions logged: native runners over cross-compilation, automated formula update inside the workflow, and formula staying in the main repo. Key prerequisite called out: repository Settings → Actions → Workflow permissions must be set to read/write.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.note: Creating .github/workflows/release.yml and updating Formula/setups.rb for Phase 1

events.details: YAML validation via Ruby: passed. cargo check: clean (no source changes). Two placeholder comment lines removed from formula. Remaining PLACEHOLDER sha256 values are intentional — CI sed substitution replaces them at release time.

events.decision: Implementation: PLACEHOLDER sha256 values retained in Formula/setups.rb; only the developer-facing comment lines removed
events.rationale: The sed commands in the publish job require the PLACEHOLDER_SHA256_AARCH64 and PLACEHOLDER_SHA256_X86_64 strings to be present as substitution targets. Removing them would make the sed commands no-ops and leave the formula without sha256 values. After the CI runs, both PLACEHOLDER values are replaced with real 64-character hex strings, satisfying the post-CI grep check.

events.summary: Created .github/workflows/release.yml with parallel native macOS build matrix (macos-14/aarch64, macos-13/x86_64) and a publish job that reads checksums, updates the formula via BSD sed, commits to master, and creates the GitHub Release with both tarballs. Removed the two placeholder comment lines from Formula/setups.rb; sha256 placeholder values retained as CI sed substitution targets.

items.description: Enable read/write workflow permissions: go to the repo Settings → Actions → General → Workflow permissions and select "Read and write permissions", then save.
Push a test tag to trigger the workflow: `git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0` (use a version that doesn't already exist on the remote).

Open the Actions tab on GitHub and confirm three jobs appear: two parallel `Build` jobs (one for `aarch64-apple-darwin`, one for `x86_64-apple-darwin`) and a `Publish release` job that starts only after both build jobs turn green.
On the GitHub Releases page for `v0.1.0`, confirm both `setups-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz` and `setups-x86_64-apple-darwin.tar.gz` appear as release assets.
After the workflow completes, run `grep PLACEHOLDER Formula/setups.rb` on the `master` branch and confirm it returns no matches (both sha256 values are 64-character hex strings).

On an Apple Silicon Mac, run `brew tap holynakamoto/setups https://github.com/holynakamoto/setups && brew install holynakamoto/setups/setups` and confirm installation completes without checksum errors.
Run `brew test holynakamoto/setups/setups` and confirm it exits 0 with output containing `setups`.

workflow.rationale: Fixes broken brew install behavior caused by missing CI and placeholder SHA256s — no new user-visible features (skip design), well-contained to 2 files in a single CI subsystem (skip architecture)

stageContent.implementation:
## Phase 1: Automated release pipeline

### Files Changed

- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — created; tag-triggered (`v*.*.*`) workflow with:
  - `build` matrix job: `macos-14` (aarch64) and `macos-13` (x86_64), each running `rustup target add`, `cargo build --release --target`, creating a `.tar.gz`, computing SHA256 via `shasum -a 256`, and uploading both as artifacts
  - `publish` job: downloads all artifacts, reads both `.sha256` files into env vars, applies BSD `sed -i ''` to replace `PLACEHOLDER_SHA256_AARCH64` and `PLACEHOLDER_SHA256_X86_64` in `Formula/setups.rb`, commits and pushes to master, then creates the GitHub Release with both tarballs as assets
  - Workflow-level `permissions: contents: write` for `GITHUB_TOKEN` push and release create

- `Formula/setups.rb` — removed the two developer-facing comment lines (`# Replace PLACEHOLDER_SHA256...` on original lines 9 and 15); `sha256 "PLACEHOLDER_SHA256_*"` values intentionally retained as sed substitution targets for the CI workflow

### Deviations from Plan

None. Implementation follows the spec exactly. The PLACEHOLDER sha256 values in the formula were kept (not removed) because the sed commands in the CI require those strings as substitution targets — this is the intended design per R7.

### Verification

- YAML valid (confirmed via `ruby -e "require 'yaml'; YAML.load_file(...)"`)
- `cargo check` clean — no Rust source changes, no regressions
- Comment lines removed from formula (grep confirmed)
- Post-CI checks (no PLACEHOLDER in formula, real sha256s, GitHub Release assets) require pushing a `v*.*.*` tag to verify
